dominant_error_pattern: Students defer precise problem definition in favor of reviewing systems data, prior interventions, or background records, misunderstanding that operational specificity is the prerequisite gating step for all consultation work.
instructional_red_flags: Distractors A, C, D all pre-empt definition. Correct answer (B) = define first. Teach: 'You cannot meaningfully review MTSS data or prior interventions until you know EXACTLY what problem you're solving. Define first: \Teases girls when?\" \"Off task how?\" Be specific before you read anything.'"

dominant_error_pattern: Students classify normal systems-level resource limitations (funding, staffing) as equivalent consultation barriers to relational and goal-clarity obstacles that directly undermine collaborative process.
instructional_red_flags: Distractors A, B, C are legitimate barriers; D (finances) may be a constraint but is not typically classified as a consultation process barrier. Teach: 'Common barriers that wreck consultation: bad relationships, unclear goals, unclear communication. These block actual problem-solving. Money is a resource issue, not a barrier to the consultation itself—you problem-solve within what you have.'

dominant_error_pattern: Students assume social-emotional, client-centered, or multilevel frameworks represent NASP-endorsed consultation best practice, misidentifying the specific emphasis on consultee-centered indirect service as the foundational recommended model.
instructional_red_flags: Distractors A, B, D name credible-sounding approaches but aren't NASP's stated endorsement. Correct answer (C) = consultee-centered. Teach: 'NASP says: consultee-centered consultation is the flagship model. Why? Because it scales. You build teacher capacity, and that capacity ripples to all future students that teacher works with. Multiply your impact.'

dominant_error_pattern: Students identify any single barrier (time, assessment prioritization, or implicit bias) as sufficient to explain system change obstacles, underestimating the compounded effect of multiple simultaneous organizational and cultural constraints.
instructional_red_flags: Distractors A, B, C each isolate one barrier; correct answer (D) = all three interact. Teach: 'System change fails when you have time pressure + a testing culture + blind spots about equity. They feed each other. Example: \I'd consult more if I had time (A)
column6: and I never notice whose kids get excluded (C).\" All three together block change.'"
